data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
08:30 AM Employment Situation

The most closely watched of all economic indicators, the employment situation is a set of monthly labor market indicators based on two separate reports: the establishment survey and the household survey which interviews 60,000 households and generates the unemployment rate.

Nonfarm jobs expected up 110K and the jobless rate at 4.3 percent for June versus 139K and 4.2 percent in May.

08:30 AM International Trade in Goods and Services

Updating the goods portion of the advance report and offering initial data on services, this report provides complete information on cross-border trade.

The trade gap is expected at $70.8 billion, after dropping to $61.6 billion in April as tariffs continue to add volatility.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims seen at 240 K versus 236 K in the previous week.

9:45 AM PMI Composite Final

US Services Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) is based on monthly questionnaire surveys collected from over 400 U.S. companies, which provide a leading indication of what is happening in the private sector services economy.

No revision expected from the flash at 53.1 in services.

10:00 AM Factory Orders

Factory orders represent the dollar level of new orders for both durable and nondurable goods. With durable goods already reported up 16.4 percent, another huge aircraft-driven rise expected at 8.1 percent on the month.

10:00 AM ISM Services Index

Producing a monthly composite on general activity tracked in volumes, the Institute for Supply Management surveys several hundred service-providing firms from 16 industries.

Slight recovery to 50.5 expected from 49.9 in May.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.

11:00 AM Fed's Bostic Speech

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will give keynote speech on US monetary policy in the IMFS Distinguished Lecture Series hosted by Institute for Monetary and Financial Stability.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Enterprise Products says U.S. lifts restrictions on ethane shipments to China
data.description:
Enterprise Products Partners (NYSE:EPD) said Wednesday the U.S. government has removed license requirements for shipping ethane to China, clearing the way for deliveries to Chinese ports without additional approvals.

A letter revoking the previous license requirement, disclosed in an 8-K filing, came after the U.S. and China resolved a dispute last week around shipments of rare earth minerals and magnets to the U.S.

The U.S. Commerce Department sent letters last week to Enterprise Products (NYSE:EPD) and Energy Transfer (NYSE:ET) telling the companies they could load ethane on vessels destined for China but could not unload the ethane in China without authorization.

About half of all U.S. exports of ethane, which is extracted from U.S. shale gas and primarily used as a petrochemical feedstock, are shipped to China, and an end to shipments would have hurt U.S. producers as well as Chinese petrochemical manufacturers.

data.title: Crude oil jumps in light pre-holiday trade despite surprise build in U.S. stockpiles
data.description:
Crude oil futures jumped Wednesday in their strongest gains in two weeks after Iran said it is suspending cooperation with the International Atomic Energy Agency, returning some of the risk premium that faded following last week’s Israel-Iran ceasefire.

President Trump said he had reached a trade agreement with Vietnam, the third announced following deals with China and the U.K. and China, adding to hopes for more agreements ahead of a July 9 deadline for the return of "reciprocal" tariffs.

The size of oil's price gains likely was exacerbated by low liquidity ahead of Friday's Fourth of July holiday in the U.S.

The market appeared to ignore a bearish report from the Energy Information Administration that showed a surprise 3.8M-barrel build in U.S. crude stocks, their first increase in six weeks, and a 1M bbl/day drop in U.S. gasoline demand.

The EIA said crude imports rose by 976K bbl/day to 6.9M while exports fell by nearly 2M bbl/day to 2.3M; U.S. crude oil production was estimated at 13.4M bbl/day, virtually unchanged from the previous week; and gasoline inventories rose by 4.2M barrels vs. expectations for a decline, as gasoline demand fell to 8.6M bbl/day from 9.7M bbl/day in the prior week.

"Any time gasoline demand is down by over a million is a negative event for the oil industry," Mizuho analyst Robert Yawger said in a note. "When it happens in the midst of summer driving season, it is a negative event with an exclamation point!"

In the coming days, investors will focus on Thursday's monthly non-farm payrolls report and this weekend's OPEC+ meeting that is widely assumed to confirm another large production increase in August.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for August delivery closed +3% to $67.45/bbl, front-month ISeptember Brent crude (CO1:COM) finished +3% to $69.11/bbl, the largest one-day percentage gain since June 17, and front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for August delivery settled +2.1% to $3.488/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Datadog surges after placement on S&P 500
data.description:
Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) leaped 9% during post-market trading on Wednesday after it was revealed the company will replace Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR) on the S&P 500 Index (SP500).

The change becomes effective on July 9. Juniper was removed due to Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE:HPE) completing its acquisition of the networking products provider.

Datadog provides software for monitoring and analytics of cloud-scale applications and infrastructure. Bank of America listed the company as one of its top 10 ideas for the third quarter.

data.title: Gold edges higher after weak ADP payroll data, trade uncertainty
data.description:
Gold futures rose Wednesday after ADP's weaker than expected payrolls data showed U.S. employers shed 33K workers last month, the first print showing net private sector job losses since March 2023, adding to investor hopes that the U.S. Federal Reserve could reduce interest rates sooner than anticipated.

Some economists said the labor market weakness could be the final piece needed to prompt the Federal Reserve to cut rates; non-interest bearing gold generally thrives in a low-interest rate environment.

The report raises the stakes for the monthly non-farm payrolls report due on Thursday for more insight into the health of the labor market; economists surveyed by Dow Jones expect to see growth of just 115K new jobs.

"If we get a much stronger than expected jobs report, that's going to be bearish [for gold] because the Fed might not be able to cut rates as soon as it wants to or as many times as it might otherwise," Kitco analyst Jim Wyckoff wrote.

Traders also are focused on tariff uncertainty, with President Trump saying he would not extend the July 9 deadline on the 90-day reprieve on his higher "reciprocal" tariffs on many countries.

Adding to investor concerns, Trump's tax and spending bill, which is projected to add $3.3T to the total U.S. debt, is headed to the House of Representatives for possible final approval.

Finally, Iran said it was suspending cooperation with the United Nations' atomic agency, which could add to geopolitical tensions with the U.S.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for July delivery ended +0.3% to $3,348.00/oz, its third consecutive daily gain, and front-month July Comex silver (XAGUSD:CUR) settled +0.9% to $36.426/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (NYSEARCA:IAU), (NYSEARCA:NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: ABM Industries upgraded at Truist Securities on turnaround potential
data.description:
ABM Industries (NYSE:ABM) could be poised for a rebound after a period of operational challenges, according to Truist Securities, which upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold on Wednesday.

The stock has underperformed the S&P 500 (SP500) by roughly 20 percentage points over the last year, dragged down by three consecutive free cash flow (FCF) misses tied to a troubled rollout of a new enterprise resource planning (ERP) system. But with those disruptions easing and expectations now reset, ABM (NYSE:ABM) appears well-positioned to outperform, Truist research analyst Jasper Bibb wrote in a note to clients on July 2.

“We think the near-term set-up is attractive enough (discount valuation and low expectations) at current levels to more than offsets our lingering skepticism,” Bibb said.

ABM (ABM), which provides facility services across sectors including aviation, commercial real estate, and manufacturing, historically was a strong generator of cash. From fiscal 2015 through 2024, the company averaged FCF conversion of 53%, but that figure turned negative on a trailing 12-month basis because of delays in billing caused by the ERP transition. Management recently indicated those billing delays are stabilizing, with invoices nearly back on schedule as of June.

“We expect FCF conversion to begin normalizing in the second half of the year, which alone should be enough to drive stock outperformance,” Bibb wrote, calling the ERP implementation a “one-time event” in an otherwise cash-generative business.

Further supporting Truist’s bullish outlook is the expectation for revenue growth to exceed current conservative forecasts. Bibb noted that consensus estimates imply organic growth will slow to around 3% in the second half, down from an estimated 5.4% in the fiscal second quarter, after adjusting for workdays.

But easy year-over-year comparisons and improving conditions in the U.S. office market could fuel stronger-than-expected growth, particularly within ABM’s Business & Industry segment, which has faced headwinds from rising office vacancy rates in recent years.

The report pointed to recent industry data from Jones Lang LaSalle showing a 15% increase in U.S. office leasing activity during the first quarter, suggesting a stabilization that could benefit ABM (ABM), whose commercial office customers account for roughly 40% of total revenue.

Truist also sees ABM’s (ABM) current valuation as appealing. The stock trades at approximately 8.4 times estimated 2025 adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, compared with its 10-year average multiple of 9.4 times. On a free cash flow basis, ABM (AMB) is priced at an 11% yield based on Truist’s 2026 forecast, with the potential for share buybacks to resume once the ERP migration is completed.

Bibb’s new $58 price target, up from $55 a share, reflects an 8.3 times multiple on estimated 2026 adjusted ebitda.

data.title: Bombardier upgraded at Scotiabank as demand rebounds, confidence grows
data.description:
Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) is gaining momentum after a period of sluggish order activity, prompting Scotiabank to upgrade its rating on the stock, citing renewed demand, easing trade uncertainty and the aviation company’s continued efforts to strengthen its balance sheet.

In a report published late Tuesday, Konark Gupta, an equity research analyst at Scotiabank, raised his recommendation on Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) to Sector Outperform from Sector Perform previously.

The upgrade comes after Gupta hosted an investor tour of Bombardier’s new Global Manufacturing Centre near Toronto Pearson International Airport, where management emphasized improved market conditions and operational progress.

“While our discussion and site visit led us to believe that the company is firing on all cylinders after a lackluster Q1 order intake, the icing on the cake was its latest significant order win, which boosts our confidence in management’s near-term and long-term outlook,” Gupta wrote.

The company's latest order for 50 jets, worth $1.7 billion, underscores renewed customer confidence, the analyst said. Including options and an accompanying service agreement, the total value of the deal exceeds $4 billion. The order came from a first-time customer and will contribute to deliveries from 2027 onward, helping to extend Bombardier’s (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) backlog beyond the typical cycle window.

The new order alone implies a book-to-bill ratio of 1.4 times for the second quarter, even before accounting for undisclosed orders, Gupta noted.

Management appears unfazed by competition from Gulfstream’s G800 and G400 programs or Dassault’s delayed Falcon 10X, according to Gupta, who also highlighted Bombardier’s growing aftermarket and defense businesses. The company remains on track to meet its 2030 revenue targets of $2.8 billion to $3.9 billion for aftermarket services and $1 billion to $1.5 billion for defense, implying significant growth compared to current levels.

Scotiabank now forecasts Bombardier’s (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) aftermarket revenue to surpass $2.2 billion this year, representing growth of more than 8% driven by increased jet utilization, an expanding installed base, and market share gains. Defense momentum is also building, supported by a recent order from Saab and partnerships with aerospace suppliers Safran and Leonardo.

The analyst pointed to Canada’s new commitment to raise defense spending to 5% of GDP by 2035 as a further catalyst for Bombardier’s defense ambitions.

In addition to operational improvements, Gupta emphasized Bombardier’s (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) “relentless focus” on reducing debt. The company is targeting a leverage ratio of 2.0 to 2.5 times by year-end, supported by both earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization growth and active debt repayment. Recent moves include redeeming part of its high-yield 7.875% notes due 2027 and issuing lower-cost debt maturing in 2033.

Gupta said Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) remains on track to achieve an investment-grade credit rating by 2027, following a recent upgrade from S&P to BB-.

Despite a 30% rally in Bombardier (OTCQX:BDRBF) (OTCQX:BDRAF) (OTCPK:BOMBF) shares over the past two months, outpacing the broader TSX’s 8% gain, Scotiabank sees further upside, noting the stock still trades at a discount to peers on valuation multiples. The firm raised its enterprise value-to-ebitda multiple for Bombardier to 8.5 times from 7 times, reflecting improved free cash flow expectations and reduced market-cycle risk.

data.title: Buy-rated financial small caps with the best near-term prospects
data.description:
So far in 2025, financial stocks have outperformed the overall market, but small caps have lagged behind. Where are the biggest opportunities among smaller players in the financial sector?

Year to date, the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLF) has climbed about 9%, with an upswing of 26% over the past 12 months. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 has advanced about 6% so far in 2025 and 13% over the past year.

In contrast, the iShares Russell 2000 ETF (NYSEARCA:IWM), which tracks the small-cap Russell 2000 index, has dipped nearly 2% in 2025. It has advanced about 9% over a 12-month span.

In an effort to find potential opportunities among smaller players in the sector, the following list contains financial stocks with a market cap of between $300M and $2B that have consensus Buy ratings from both SA analysts and Wall Street analysts. The names on the list are ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings. This system grades stocks on their relative performance on certain key quantitative measures, like profitability, valuation and momentum. 

Here are the top quant-rated small-cap financial stocks that also have Buy ratings from both SA and Wall Street analysts:

1. Heritage Insurance Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:HRTG) -- Quant Rating: 4.96; Market Cap: $701.42M

2. OppFi Inc. (NYSE:OPFI) -- Quant Rating: 4.96; Market Cap: $1.18B

3. Waterdrop Inc. (NYSE:WDH) -- Quant Rating: 4.86; Market Cap: $488.24M

4. Intercorp Financial Services Inc. (NYSE:IFS) -- Quant Rating: 4.82; Market Cap: $1.20B

5. Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. (NYSE:MCB) -- Quant Rating: 4.78; Market Cap: $770.09M

6. Tompkins Financial Corp. (NYSE:TMP) -- Quant Rating: 4.75; Market Cap: $931.74M

7. Northrim BanCorp, Inc. (NASDAQ:NRIM) -- Quant Rating: 4.72; Market Cap: $528.10M

8. HBT Financial, Inc. (NASDAQ:HBT) Quant Rating: 4.70; Market Cap: $817.15M

9. Peoples Financial Services Corp. (NASDAQ:PFIS) -- Quant Rating: 4.66; Market Cap: $513.23M

10. OFG Bancorp (NYSE:OFG) -- Quant Rating: 4.56; Market Cap: $1.97B

data.title: Railroads gear up for Q2 earnings amid tariff, coal pressures
data.description:
With the railroad sector set to report Q2 results in the coming weeks, UBS examines how the coal industry, natural gas prices, and import tariffs are shaping the performance and outlook of the three major railroad operators.

Norfolk Southern

Ranked as the top railroad operator by Seeking Alpha’s Quant ranking, Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) is best positioned to see upward 2025 EPS revisions as it appears the company “can better absorb weaker export coal volume and yield,” UBS analyst Thomas Wadewitz writes in his research note.

“Norfolk Southern has several tailwinds going for it,” agrees Seeking Alpha analyst Kody’s Dividends. “For one, the company’s industrial development pipeline closed out Q1 2025 at an historic high.”

The company’s PSR 2.0 Transformation program is another positive with $292M in cost savings and productivity gains, with an additional $150M projected in 2025. 

Accordingly, Wadewitz raises his EPS estimate for the quarter to $3.35 from $3.27 versus the Seeking Alpha consensus estimate of $3.26. Wadewitz also targets a 63.1% operating ratio thanks to the company’s productivity gains and sees further upside potential in the EPS outlook as positive mix and the company’s strong cost side performance should continue.  

Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) reports Q2 results during regular market hours on July 29.

Union Pacific

Union Pacific Corp (NYSE:UNP) also has the greatest potential for upward EPS revisions thanks to expectations for stronger than expected coal and grain volumes, as well as cost performance from lower purchased services and rents, all of which should translate into an operating ratio of 58.6% for the second quarter.

UBS’s Wadewitz models Q2 coal volume at +30% year-over-year and grain volume at +8.5% for his increased EPS estimates, now pegged at $2.94 from $2.87, as well as an increase to 2025 EPS estimate to $11.60 from $11.40, reflecting 20% growth in coal volume and strong cost controls.

“The company maintains high margins and operational efficiency, which offsets weak segments with strong grain and container volumes, and disciplined cost control,” Seeking Alpha analyst Gevork Avtandilov writes.

For this reason, and with “robust” shareholder returns, Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating ranks Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) third among its six ranked railroad operators.

Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) reports Q2 results before the open on July 24.

CSX

Near the bottom of Seeking Alpha’s Quant ranking is CSX (NASDAQ:CSX), which serves a diversified set of industrial and consumer markets through its 19K route miles east of the Mississippi. Unfortunately, continued merchandise volume softness coupled with the delayed start of the Leer South coal mine led UBS to lower their Q2 EPS estimates by a penny to $0.43 per share.

Additionally, this volume mix will also serve as a headwind due to tariff-imposed weakness in merchandise volumes.

And unlike competitors, CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) faces significant yield/cost headwinds which will negatively impact FY25/26 EBITDA.

“Near-term yield/cost headwinds should largely offset solid core volumes,” says Seeking Alpha analyst Moretus Research.

“Despite network normalization and timing of new industrial-development site volumes supporting ~2.5% FY25E carload growth, depressed coal revenue/unit is driving a blended -1% FY25E yield headwind, while export coal/fuel repricing and the Howard St. Tunnel reroute are creating a combined +$300mn revenue/expense drag in 2025 – cost headwinds we believe are under-appreciated by consensus.”

CSX (NASDAQ:CSX) reports second quarter results after the close on July 23.
